disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden, severe tearing/ripping chest pain radiating straight through to the interscapular back, onset ~30 minutes ago
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a tearing, ripping pain right through my chest and it shoots straight through to my back, between my shoulder blades. It started suddenly about half an hour ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain began abruptly during physical exertion (moving boxes)
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when it started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was moving some boxes and it hit me all of a sudden.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe pounding headache and feeling faint accompanying the chest pain
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any other symptoms?
How is your head, do you feel faint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My head is absolutely pounding and I feel like I could pass out.

trigger_question_examples: How is your breathing right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can barely catch my breath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known Turner syndrome with a bicuspid aortic valve and a coarctation of the aorta (patient's stated lifelong history)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I have Turner syndrome, and I was born with a narrowing in my aorta (a coarctation) and a bicuspid aortic valve.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Takes a daily beta-blocker for blood pressure and took it this morning
trigger_question_examples: Do you take any regular medications?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I take a daily beta-blocker tablet for my blood pressure and I took it this morning.
